Overview

Address
201 VILLAGE DRIVE, CANONSBURG, PA 15317

Setting
Urban
1 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 1/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 1/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 2/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 1/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 1/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures — Data not available.

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 3.21 3.75 3.31 ≥ 3.48 Below floor
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.82 0.66 0.84 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.57 0.83 0.59 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 1.82 2.26 1.87 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.39 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.21 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
2.85
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.57
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
2.94
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
65.7%
annual
RN turnover
87.5%
annual
Administrators departed
1
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.33
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.96
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-02-10 26 12 14 515 3 876
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2023-12-08 20 10 11 128 1 128
Total weighted health score
689.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (52)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

48 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
1 G/H/I Actual harm
3 J/K/L Immediate jeopardy
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2026-01-23 —
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. D 2026-01-23 —
0569 Notify each resident of certain balances and convey resident funds upon discharge, eviction, or death. E 2025-09-30 2025-11-21
0575 Post a list of names, addresses, and telephone numbers of all pertinent State agencies and advocacy groups and a statement that the resident may file a complaint with the State Survey Agency. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0585 Honor the resident's right to voice grievances without discrimination or reprisal and the facility must establish a grievance policy and make prompt efforts to resolve grievances. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. K 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. K 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. K 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0835 Administer the facility in a manner that enables it to use its resources effectively and efficiently. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0865 Have a plan that describes the process for conducting QAPI and QAA activities. E 2025-09-12 2025-10-14
0558 Reasonably accommodate the needs and preferences of each resident. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0580 Immediately tell the resident, the resident's doctor, and a family member of situations (injury/decline/room, etc.) that affect the resident. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. G 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0641 Ensure each resident receives an accurate assessment. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0684 Provide appropriate treatment and care according to orders, resident’s preferences and goals. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0883 Develop and implement policies and procedures for flu and pneumonia vaccinations. D 2025-02-10 2025-03-27
0760 Ensure that residents are free from significant medication errors. D 2024-09-07 2024-10-01
0565 Honor the resident's right to organize and participate in resident/family groups in the facility. E 2024-07-18 2024-07-31
0679 Provide activities to meet all resident's needs. E 2024-07-18 2024-07-31
0680 Ensure the activities program is directed by a qualified professional. E 2024-07-18 2024-07-31
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. D 2024-06-24 2024-07-15
0584 Honor the resident's right to a safe, clean, comfortable and homelike environment, including but not limited to receiving treatment and supports for daily living safely. E 2024-03-04 2024-03-19
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2024-03-04 2024-03-19
0836 Ensure the facility is licensed under applicable State and local law and operates and provides services in compliance with all applicable Federal, State, and local laws, regulations, and codes, and with accepted professional standards. E 2023-12-28 2024-01-23
